name: factory-skill-evals
description: Behavioral evals for the factory skill

evals.name: End-to-end on sample spec
evals.description: Factory run on the trivial sample spec produces a branch with implementation and a PR
assertions: A git branch named factory/{work-id} exists with commits
The implementation matches the spec (greet.sh created and functional)
A manifest.json is written to .factory/{work-id}/
INSPECT subagent returns a verdict (APPROVE or REWORK)
If approved, a PR is created via gh

evals.name: Rework loop fires on deliberate bug
evals.description: INSPECT catches an issue and BUILD fixes it in a subsequent attempt
assertions: manifest.json shows at least 2 attempts
First attempt inspect verdict is REWORK with specific items
Second attempt addresses the rework items
Final status is approved or escalated

evals.name: Rework limit enforced at 3 attempts
evals.description: After 3 failed BUILD-INSPECT cycles, the factory escalates instead of looping forever
assertions: manifest.json shows exactly 3 attempts
Final status is escalated
No PR is created
User is informed of the escalation with last rework items

evals.name: Manifest records full attempt history
evals.description: Every BUILD and INSPECT cycle is recorded in the manifest with structured data
assertions: manifest.json exists at .factory/{work-id}/manifest.json
Each attempt has build.files_changed, build.tests, build.commit
Each attempt has inspect.verdict and inspect.summary
metrics.cycle_time_seconds is a positive number
metrics.first_pass_yield is a boolean

evals.name: INSPECT has fresh context (no builder bias)
evals.description: The INSPECT subagent is spawned without worktree isolation and without the BUILD conversation context
assertions: INSPECT subagent prompt contains only the spec and diff reference, not BUILD reasoning
INSPECT is spawned as a separate Agent call (not resumed from BUILD)
INSPECT does not use isolation: worktree

evals.name: Concurrent runs don't interfere
evals.description: Two factory runs with different specs use separate work IDs and branches
assertions: Each run has a unique work ID (different timestamps)
Each run uses a different branch (factory/{work-id-1} vs factory/{work-id-2})
Each run has its own manifest directory

evals.name: FeatureBench: Seaborn Regression Plotting (5 files, 30k chars)
evals.description: Real-world benchmark task from FeatureBench (ICLR 2026). Medium-complexity task implementing statistical regression plotting with multiple model types. Tests BUILD implementation quality and INSPECT statistical review rigor.
assertions: BUILD creates regression.py with statistical computation
INSPECT reviews statistical correctness (bootstrap, CI, fitting)
INSPECT checks edge case handling (NaN, empty, singleton)
Rework items are specific and actionable if REWORK verdict
Manifest records all attempts with build/inspect details
Factory produces a branch with implementation commits
